target_question: Cohort-level DCST structure: does HMP2 show a nontrivial depth-1 and depth-2 DCST landscape rather than collapsing into noise?
replication_criterion: Same pipeline runs cleanly and yields interpretable depth-1 and depth-2 DCST distributions
notes: Structural validation does not require exact taxon proportions to match AGP.

target_question: Combined IBD vs healthy: do inflammatory disease samples show distinct DCST enrichments or depletions relative to healthy controls?
replication_criterion: Directionally similar enrichment/depletion for key IBD-related DCSTs or parent groups

target_question: Crohn vs UC: do CD and UC separate at depth 1 or depth 2 in a biologically interpretable way?
replication_criterion: At least one reproducible CD-vs-UC contrast at depth 1 or depth 2
notes: HMP2 metadata appears suitable for this.

target_question: Depth-2 heterogeneity: do large parent DCSTs split into subtypes with distinct IBD associations?
replication_criterion: At least one parent DCST shows meaningful subtype-specific IBD or CD/UC differences
notes: This is one of the most valuable validation questions.

target_question: Inflammation-associated expansion: are Morganella / Enterobacterales / related inflammatory dominance patterns enriched in IBD?
replication_criterion: Directional enrichment in IBD even if exact taxa or effect sizes differ
notes: Small-cell issues may limit exact Morganella replication.

target_question: Protective commensal framing: are Faecalibacterium-associated patterns relatively depleted in IBD or healthier samples?
replication_criterion: Directional protective/depleted pattern consistent with discovery and literature
notes: Good bridge between discovery findings and established IBD literature.

target_question: Bacteroides nuance: do Bacteroides-related dominant or subtype patterns show heterogeneous IBD association rather than one uniform signal?
replication_criterion: Evidence that not all Bacteroides-linked states behave the same
notes: Important to avoid overbroad Bacteroides claims.

target_question: Prevotella nuance: does HMP2 provide any usable evidence on Prevotella lineage-specific behavior in IBD?
replication_criterion: Directional signal if sample counts permit
notes: Useful if present, but not a must-have for Phase 2 success.

target_question: Escherichia-Shigella / Enterobacteriaceae dysbiosis: does a similar gastrointestinal inflammatory signal appear despite taxonomy caveats?
replication_criterion: Directional inflammatory enrichment with explicit taxonomy caveat
notes: Interpret with care; exact genus naming may differ.

target_question: Non-IBD AGP phenotype replication: IBS, reflux, allergies, migraine, obesity, kidney disease, CVD
notes: HMP2 should not be used to judge these claims.
